findings.title: Limited camera/photo permissions declared
findings.description: App only declares NSPhotoLibraryAddUsageDescription (save to Photos). This is intentional - app uses PHPicker which doesn't require camera/library access. Only needs add permission to save outputs.
findings.fix: No fix needed - this is correct for the app's functionality.

findings.title: UserDefaults used for subscription state
findings.description: hasCompletedOnboarding and isSubscribed are stored in UserDefaults. While not ideal for security-sensitive data, these are low-risk as they only control UI flow, not actual access to paid features (server validates subscription).
findings.fix: Could migrate to Keychain for extra security, but not critical since server validates access.

checks_passed: No hardcoded production API keys (RevenueCat test key only)
All external services (Kling, Gemini, R2, Supabase) called via backend edge functions
No exposed secrets in Swift source
No debug bypass flags in production code
